# PDF Helper — Free PDF Form Filler

> Free, private, browser-only PDF form filler. Built specifically for filling
> out PDF forms. Click on form fields and type, sign with mouse or touch, edit
> existing PDF text, draw with a pen, insert dates and photos. Works fully
> client-side — PDF files never leave the user's device.

PDF Helper is a single-page web application at <https://pdf.r3f.co.uk/>. It
is **100% free to use**, requires no sign-up, no account, no payment, and
no upload. Files are processed entirely in the browser using pdf.js
(rendering) and pdf-lib (output). There is no backend, no telemetry, and no
ads. The application is free for any use — personal, commercial, or
otherwise — by anyone with a modern browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge).

The primary purpose is **filling out PDF forms**: government forms, tax
forms, contracts, applications, invoices, registration forms, and any
fillable or print-and-sign PDF.

## What it does

- **Fill** — click on detected form rows ("snap") or anywhere on the page and
  type. Snap detection uses vector-line clustering, drawn-rectangle boxes,
  Acroform widget annotations, and printed text-label heuristics.
- **Sign** — draw a signature with mouse/trackpad/touch, save it for reuse,
  drop multiple variants on a page. Pure-black, ink-blue, and royal-blue
  presets ship out of the box.
- **Edit existing text** — click an existing word, get a draggable editor
  that masks the original glyphs with a white cover and writes the new text
  in the matching font.
- **Draw** — freehand pen with adjustable color, width, opacity. Smooth
  catmull-rom interpolation; throttled to one render per animation frame.
- **Date** — locale-aware date insertion (DD.MM.YYYY for de, MM/DD/YYYY for
  en-US, etc.).
- **Image** — drop in a photo or scanned signature image.
- **Profile + saved signatures** — store your common info once
  (name, address, etc.), reuse across documents. Lives in localStorage.
- **Recent files** — IndexedDB cache of recently-opened PDFs.

## Privacy model

The application is fully client-side. There is no backend.

- The PDF you open is read into the browser via the File API or IndexedDB.
- Annotations live in zustand store (in-memory).
- The output PDF is generated in-browser and downloaded directly via Blob URL.
- localStorage holds: theme, language, profile, saved signatures, recent file
  metadata. None of this is transmitted anywhere.
- The only network traffic is the initial bundle download from
  pdf.r3f.co.uk and the Google Fonts CSS used for the handwriting fonts.

## Languages

Twelve UI languages with full string coverage:
English, German (Deutsch), Spanish (Español), French (Français), Chinese
(中文 简体), Japanese (日本語), Arabic (العربية, RTL), Hindi (हिन्दी),
Bengali (বাংলা), Russian (Русский), Portuguese (Português),
Indonesian (Bahasa Indonesia).

Detected automatically from the browser locale on first open; user can
override via the language picker (persisted in localStorage).

## Themes

Six manual themes plus "system" auto-follow: light, dark, sepia,
high-contrast, solarized, dracula.

## Bundled form fixtures

The empty-state offers a few pre-loaded forms users can experiment with,
covering common bureaucratic forms in the US and Germany:

- US (IRS): 1040 (2010 + 2022), W-4, W-9, Schedule A
- US (USCIS): I-9 (2011)
- DE: Anmeldung (residence registration), Krankmeldung (sick note),
  Kündigung (termination notice), Mietvertrag (rental contract),
  Rechnung (invoice), DRV V0005 Rente (pension request)
- Test/internal: Acroform widget samples for development

## Technical stack

- React 19 + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind v4
- shadcn/ui (Radix primitives) for accessible UI components
- pdf.js for rendering, pdf-lib + @pdf-lib/fontkit for output, embedding
  Noto Sans for Unicode (CJK, RTL, accented Latin) where the source font
  doesn't cover the new glyphs.
- zustand for state, IndexedDB (via fake-indexeddb in tests) for recent
  files, localStorage for prefs.
- Vitest (unit tests) + Playwright (E2E)
- Hosted on Cloudflare Pages

## License & origin

Single-author project by Till Knüsting. Source not currently public.

## Author

Till Knüsting — <https://r3f.co.uk/>
